opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm 32 weeks pregnant and I'm really scared — I'm weeing every hour, the thrush down below is unbearable, and the baby hasn't been moving as much as usual today.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The thrush and the constant weeing have been getting worse over the last couple of days, and this evening I noticed the baby feels quieter than normal, which frightened me enough to call.

symptom_narrative.onset: the itching and discharge started about 2 days ago; the heavy weeing and thirst have built up over the past week or so; the baby has felt quieter since earlier today
symptom_narrative.duration: about 48 hours for the thrush; the quieter movements only today
symptom_narrative.character: severe itching and a thick white discharge, constant thirst, going to the toilet all the time including waking about 6 times overnight, and the baby's kicks feel fewer and softer than usual
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itching is unbearable and the tiredness from being up all night is wearing me down, but what really scares me is the baby being quiet
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the itching and discharge are down below; the thirst and weeing are constant; the baby feels quiet low in my belly
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the weeing and thirst are constant day and night; the quieter movements have been through this evening
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped the itching or the weeing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the itching feels worse when I'm hot and the weeing keeps me from sleeping
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: very thirsty all the time, exhausted from not sleeping, and feeling I've put on a lot of weight quickly this past month
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no thrush like this before in the pregnancy
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: not sleeping because of getting up to wee, and too anxious about the baby to settle

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: at my 28-week pregnancy test they said my sugar was a bit high / borderline and told me to watch my diet
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: about 4 weeks ago at the 28-week screen

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: otherwise a healthy first pregnancy, no diabetes or high blood pressure before pregnancy

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: first pregnancy at 32 weeks, partner at home and present for the call, very anxious about the baby

objective_data_offered.value: the baby has moved less than usual today — I'm not getting the normal strong kicks I'd expect
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective maternal perception of movements; not a substitute for a formal fetal heart-rate trace (CTG)

objective_data_offered.value: I feel like I've put on a lot of weight this past month, more than the midwife said to expect
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-estimated, no home scale reading given

objective_data_offered.value: I'm passing urine about every hour and waking around 6 times overnight, and I'm always thirsty
